Package org.areslib.hardware.coprocessors

Class OctoQuadFWv3

java.lang.Object
com.qualcomm.robotcore.hardware.I2cDeviceSynchDevice<com.qualcomm.robotcore.hardware.I2cDeviceSynchSimple>
org.areslib.hardware.coprocessors.OctoQuadFWv3
All Implemented Interfaces:
com.qualcomm.robotcore.hardware.HardwareDevice, com.qualcomm.robotcore.hardware.usb.RobotArmingStateNotifier.Callback
@I2cDeviceType @DeviceProperties(xmlTag="OctoQuadFWv3", name="OctoQuadFWv3") public class OctoQuadFWv3 extends com.qualcomm.robotcore.hardware.I2cDeviceSynchDevice<com.qualcomm.robotcore.hardware.I2cDeviceSynchSimple>

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    OctoQuadFWv3.ChannelBankConfig
     
    static class 
    OctoQuadFWv3.ChannelPulseWidthParams
    A data block containing minimum and maximum pulse widths to be applied to a channel using setSingleChannelPulseWidthParams(int, ChannelPulseWidthParams)
    static class 
    OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDataBlock
    A data block holding ALL encoder data; this block may be bulk read in one I2C operation.
    static enum 
    OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDirection
    Allows reversing an encoder channel internally on the OctoQuad.
    static class 
    OctoQuadFWv3.FirmwareVersion
     
    static enum 
    OctoQuadFWv3.I2cRecoveryMode
     
    static class 
    OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerDataBlock
    A data block holding ALL localizer data needed for navigation; this block may be bulk read in one I2C read operation.
    static enum 
    OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerStatus
    Enum representing the status of the absolute localizer algorithm You can poll this to determine when IMU calibration has finished.
    static enum 
    OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerYawAxis
    The absolute localizer automagically selects the appropriate axis on the IMU to use for yaw, regardless of physical mounting orientation.
    static class 
    OctoQuadFWv3.OctoQuadException

    void
    setChannelBankConfig(OctoQuadFWv3.ChannelBankConfig config)
    Configures the OctoQuad's channel banks This parameter will NOT be retained across power cycles, unless you call saveParametersToFlash() ()}

    setI2cRecoveryMode(OctoQuadFWv3.I2cRecoveryMode mode)
    Configures the OctoQuad to use the specified I2C recovery mode.
    void
    setLocalizerCountsPerMmX(float ticksPerMmX)
    Set the scalar for converting encoder counts on the X port to millimeters of travel You SHOULD NOT calculate this - this is a real world not a theoretical one - you should measure this by pushing your robot N millimeters and dividing the counts by N.
    void
    setLocalizerCountsPerMmY(float ticksPerMmY)
    Set the scalar for converting encoder counts on the Y port to millimeters of travel You SHOULD NOT calculate this - this is a real world not a theoretical one - you should measure this by pushing your robot N millimeters and dividing the counts by N.
    void
    setLocalizerHeading(float headingRad)
    Teleport the localizer heading to a new orientation.
    void
    setLocalizerImuHeadingScalar(float headingScalar)
    Set a scale factor to apply to the IMU heading to improve accuracy The recommended way to tune this is to use the heading scalar tuning OpMode.

    Set the port index to be used by the absolute localizer routine for measuring X movement
    void
    setLocalizerPortY(int port)

    Set the direction for a single encoder This has basically the same effect as negating pos/vel in user code, except for when using the absolute localizer, in which case this must be used to inform the firmware of the tracking wheel directions.

    • setSingleEncoderDirection

      public void setSingleEncoderDirection(int idx, OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDirection direction)
      Set the direction for a single encoder This has basically the same effect as negating pos/vel in user code, except for when using the absolute localizer, in which case this must be used to inform the firmware of the tracking wheel directions. This parameter will NOT be retained across power cycles, unless you call saveParametersToFlash() ()}
      Parameters:
      idx - the index of the encoder
      direction - direction

    • getSingleEncoderDirection

      public OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDirection getSingleEncoderDirection(int idx)
      Get the direction for a single encoder
      Parameters:
      idx - the index of the encoder
      Returns:
      direction of the encoder in question

      public void setChannelBankConfig(OctoQuadFWv3.ChannelBankConfig config)
      Configures the OctoQuad's channel banks This parameter will NOT be retained across power cycles, unless you call saveParametersToFlash() ()}
      Parameters:
      config - the channel bank configuration to use

    • getChannelBankConfig

      public OctoQuadFWv3.ChannelBankConfig getChannelBankConfig()
      Queries the OctoQuad to determine the current channel bank configuration
      Returns:

    • readAllEncoderData

      public void readAllEncoderData(OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDataBlock out)
      Reads ALL encoder data from the OctoQuad, writing the data into an existing OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDataBlock object. The previous values are destroyed.
      Parameters:
      out - the OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDataBlock object to fill with new data

    • readAllEncoderData

      public OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDataBlock readAllEncoderData()

      Returns:

    • setSingleChannelPulseWidthParams

      public void setSingleChannelPulseWidthParams(int idx, int min, int max)
      Configure the minimum/maximum pulse width reported by an absolute encoder which is connected to a given channel, to allow the ability to provide accurate velocity data. These parameters will NOT be retained across power cycles, unless you call saveParametersToFlash() ()}
      Parameters:
      idx - the channel in question
      min - minimum pulse width
      max - maximum pulse width

    • setSingleChannelPulseWidthParams

      public void setSingleChannelPulseWidthParams(int idx, OctoQuadFWv3.ChannelPulseWidthParams params)
      Configure the minimum/maximum pulse width reported by an absolute encoder which is connected to a given channel, to allow the ability to provide accurate velocity data. These parameters will NOT be retained across power cycles, unless you call saveParametersToFlash() ()}
      Parameters:
      idx - the channel in question
      params - minimum/maximum pulse width

    • getSingleChannelPulseWidthParams

      public OctoQuadFWv3.ChannelPulseWidthParams getSingleChannelPulseWidthParams(int idx)
      Queries the OctoQuad to determine the currently set minimum/maxiumum pulse width for an encoder channel, to allow sane velocity data.
      Parameters:
      idx - the channel in question
      Returns:
      minimum/maximum pulse width

    • setSingleChannelPulseWidthTracksWrap

      public void setSingleChannelPulseWidthTracksWrap(int idx, boolean trackWrap)
      Configure whether in PWM mode, a channel will report the raw PWM length in microseconds, or whether it will perform "wrap tracking" for use with an absolute encoder to turn the absolute position into a continuous value.

      This is useful if you want your absolute encoder to track position across multiple rotations. NB: in order to get sane data, you MUST set the channel min/max pulse width parameter. Do not assume these values are the same for each encoder, even if they are from the same production run! REV Through Bore encoders have been observed to vary +/- 10uS from the spec'd values. You need to actually test each encoder manually by turning it very slowly until it gets to the wrap around point, and making note of the max/min values that it flips between, and then program those values into the respective channel's min/max pulse width parameter.

      Note that when using a PWM channel in "wrap tracking" mode, issuing a reset to that channel does NOT clear the position register to zero. Rather, it zeros the "accumulator" (i.e. the number of "wraps") such that immediately after issuing a reset command, the position register will contain the raw pulse width in microseconds. This behavior is chosen because it maintains the ability of the absolute encoder to actually report its absolute position. If resetting the channel were to really zero out the reported position register, using an absolute encoder would not behave any differently than using a relative encoder.

      Parameters:
      idx - the channel in question
      trackWrap - whether to track wrap around

    • getSingleChannelPulseWidthTracksWrap

      public boolean getSingleChannelPulseWidthTracksWrap(int idx)
      Get whether PWM wrap tracking is enabled for a single channel
      Parameters:
      idx - the channel in question
      Returns:
      whether PWM wrap tracking is enabled

    • setLocalizerCountsPerMmX

      public void setLocalizerCountsPerMmX(float ticksPerMmX)
      Set the scalar for converting encoder counts on the X port to millimeters of travel You SHOULD NOT calculate this - this is a real world not a theoretical one - you should measure this by pushing your robot N millimeters and dividing the counts by N.

      NOTE: this will not take effect until a call to resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU()

      Parameters:
      ticksPerMmX - scalar for converting encoder counts on the X port to millimeters of travel

    • setLocalizerCountsPerMmY

      public void setLocalizerCountsPerMmY(float ticksPerMmY)
      Set the scalar for converting encoder counts on the Y port to millimeters of travel You SHOULD NOT calculate this - this is a real world not a theoretical one - you should measure this by pushing your robot N millimeters and dividing the counts by N.

      NOTE: this will not take effect until a call to resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU()

      Parameters:
      ticksPerMmY - scalar for converting encoder counts on the Y port to millimeters of travel

    • setLocalizerTcpOffsetMmX

      public void setLocalizerTcpOffsetMmX(float tcpOffsetMmX)
      The real TCP (Tracking Center Point) of your robot is the point on the robot, where, if the robot rotates about that point, neither the X nor Y tracking wheels will rotate. This point can be determined by drawing imaginary lines parallel to, and through the middle of, your tracking wheels, and finding where those lines intersect.

      Without setting any TCP offset, if the robot rotates, the reported XY vales from the localizer will change during rotation, unless the rotation is about the real TCP. Most users will find this behavior rather unhelpful, since usually a robot will robot about its geometric center, and not about the real TCP.

      You can move the location of the localizer's "virtual" TCP away from the true location (e.g. to the center of your robot) by applying an offset. This value does not need to be extremely accurate since it will not affect the accuracy or repeatability of the localizer algorithm.

      NOTE: this will not take effect until a call to resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU()

      Parameters:
      tcpOffsetMmX - X component of the offset vector

    • setLocalizerTcpOffsetMmY

      public void setLocalizerTcpOffsetMmY(float tcpOffsetMmY)
      The real TCP (Tracking Center Point) of your robot is the point on the robot, where, if the robot rotates about that point, neither the X nor Y tracking wheels will rotate. This point can be determined by drawing imaginary lines parallel to, and through the middle of, your tracking wheels, and finding where those lines intersect.

      Without setting any TCP offset, if the robot rotates, the reported XY vales from the localizer will change during the rotation, unless the rotation is about the real TCP. Most users will find this behavior rather unhelpful, since usually a robot will robot about its geometric center, and not about the real TCP.

      You can move the location of the localizer's "virtual" TCP away from the true location (e.g. to the center of your robot) by applying an offset. This value does not need to be extremely accurate since it will not affect the accuracy or repeatability of the localizer algorithm.

      NOTE: this will not take effect until a call to resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU()

      Parameters:
      tcpOffsetMmY - Y component of the offset vector

    • setLocalizerImuHeadingScalar

      public void setLocalizerImuHeadingScalar(float headingScalar)
      Set a scale factor to apply to the IMU heading to improve accuracy The recommended way to tune this is to use the heading scalar tuning OpMode.

      NOTE: this will not take effect until a call to resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU()

      Parameters:
      headingScalar - scale factor to apply to the IMU heading to improve accuracy

    • setLocalizerPortX

      public void setLocalizerPortX(int port)
      Set the port index to be used by the absolute localizer routine for measuring X movement

      NOTE: this will not take effect until a call to resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU()

      Parameters:
      port - the port index to be used by the absolute localizer routine for measuring X movement

    • setLocalizerPortY

      public void setLocalizerPortY(int port)
      Set the port index to be used by the absolute localizer routine for measuring Y movement

      NOTE: this will not take effect until a call to resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU()

      Parameters:
      port - the port index to be used by the absolute localizer routine for measuring Y movement

    • setLocalizerVelocityIntervalMS

      public void setLocalizerVelocityIntervalMS(int ms)
      Set the period of translational velocity calculation. Longer periods give higher resolution with more latency, shorter periods give lower resolution with less latency.

      NOTE: this will not take effect until a call to resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU()

      Parameters:
      ms - 1-255ms

    • setAllLocalizerParameters

      public void setAllLocalizerParameters(int portX, int portY, float ticksPerMmX, float ticksPerMmY, float tcpOffsetMmX, float tcpOffsetMmY, float headingScalar, int velocityIntervalMs)
      Set ALL localizer parameters with one function call

      NOTE: this will not take effect until a call to resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU()

      Parameters:
      portX - see desc. for specific function call
      portY - see desc. for specific function call
      ticksPerMmX - see desc. for specific function call
      ticksPerMmY - see desc. for specific function call
      tcpOffsetMmX - see desc. for specific function call
      tcpOffsetMmY - see desc. for specific function call
      headingScalar - see desc. for specific function call
      velocityIntervalMs - see desc. for specific function call

    • getLocalizerStatus

      public OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerStatus getLocalizerStatus()
      Get the current status of the localizer algorithm
      Returns:
      current status of the localizer algorithm

    • getLocalizerHeadingAxisChoice

      public OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerYawAxis getLocalizerHeadingAxisChoice()
      Query which IMU axis the localizer decided to use for heading
      Returns:
      which IMU axis the localizer decided to use for heading

    • resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU

      public void resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU()
      Reset the localizer pose to (0,0,0) and recalibrate the IMU. Poll getLocalizerStatus() for OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerStatus.RUNNING to determine when the reset is complete.

    • readLocalizerData

      public void readLocalizerData(OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerDataBlock out)
      Bulk read ALL localizer data in one operation for maximum efficiency, writing the data into an existing OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerDataBlock object. The previous values are destroyed.
      Parameters:
      out - the OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerDataBlock object to fill with new data

    • readLocalizerData

      public OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerDataBlock readLocalizerData()
      Bulk read ALL localizer data in one operation for maximum efficiency
      Returns:
      newly read localizer data

    • readLocalizerDataAndAllEncoderData

      public void readLocalizerDataAndAllEncoderData(OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerDataBlock localizerOut, OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDataBlock encoderOut)
      Bulk read ALL localizer data and encoder data in one operation for maximum efficiency, writing the data into existing objects. The previous values are destroyed.
      Parameters:
      localizerOut - the OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerDataBlock object to fill with new localizer data
      encoderOut - the OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDataBlock object to fill with new encoder data

    • setLocalizerPose

      public void setLocalizerPose(int posXmm, int posYmm, float headingRad)
      "Teleport" the localizer to a new location. This may be useful, for instance, for updating your position based on vision targeting.
      Parameters:
      posXmm - x position in millimeters
      posYmm - y position in millimeters
      headingRad - heading in radians

    • setLocalizerHeading

      public void setLocalizerHeading(float headingRad)
      Teleport the localizer heading to a new orientation. This may be useful, for instance, for updating heading based on vision targeting.
      Parameters:
      headingRad - heading in radians

    • setI2cRecoveryMode

      public void setI2cRecoveryMode(OctoQuadFWv3.I2cRecoveryMode mode)
      Configures the OctoQuad to use the specified I2C recovery mode. This parameter will NOT be retained across power cycles, unless you call saveParametersToFlash() ()}
      Parameters:
      mode - the recovery mode to use

    • getI2cRecoveryMode

      public OctoQuadFWv3.I2cRecoveryMode getI2cRecoveryMode()
      Queries the OctoQuad to determine the currently configured I2C recovery mode
      Returns:
      the currently configured I2C recovery mode

    • saveParametersToFlash

      public void saveParametersToFlash()
      Stores the current state of parameters to flash, to be applied at next boot

    • resetEverything

      public void resetEverything()
      Run the firmware's internal reset routine
